_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Charlotte NC City Council batch 2 (5): Owens, Slack-Mayfield, Graham, Perkins-Johnson, Watlington. Evidence-based local-tier finalization. Roster web-verified June 2026 (all five won the Nov 4 2025 city election; Owens is a freshman sworn in Dec 1 2025 — first Democrat ever to win District 6). Scores reset to null where no evidence; FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/endorsement exists; TRUE only for documented alignment. Where a roll call is established by elimination from a complete named-dissenter list, the note says so.

profile.religion: United Methodist (attends, per branch.vote candidate profile)
profile.background: Attorney (UNC law; ~30 yrs practice, former Fortune-100 general-counsel work; Senior Counsel at Seyfarth Shaw); 30+ year South Charlotte resident; chaired boards of RAIN and the Havens; first public office — won District 6 Nov 4 2025, 56.8%–43.0% over Krista Bokhari, the first Democrat ever to win the district; sworn in Dec 1 2025.

kimberly-owens.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: won Nov 4 2025 (WFAE; Ballotpedia — term ends Dec 2027); seat previously held by Republican Tariq Bokhari, who resigned summer 2025. HARD evidence: 'I am unwavering in my support of law enforcement' — her own words in the WSOC Political Beat candidate guide — affirmative advocacy, so public_justice q0 TRUE. Campaign endorsements page lists Equality NC and LGBTQ+ Democrats of Mecklenburg (single-issue LGBT-advocacy endorsements; biblical_marriage q4 FALSE) and Moms Demand Action + Everytown (self_defense q1 FALSE). Voted YES on the June 8 2026 FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent property-tax increase — passed 9-2, only Driggs and Mayfield voting no, so her yes is by elimination (WSOC, Charlotte Stories; economic_stewardship FALSE). Not on council for the Nov 2025 ICE-operation responses (sworn in Dec 1) — border cells null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, school choice/parental rights, public prayer, election administration, or state-overreach cells — null, not penalized.

4.note: Campaign endorsements page lists Equality NC and LGBTQ+ Democrats of Mecklenburg County — accepted single-issue LGBT-advocacy endorsements.

2.note: Voted YES on FY2027 $4.5B budget with 1.89-cent property-tax increase, June 8 2026 (passed 9-2; only no votes Driggs and Mayfield — yes by elimination).

0.note: Direct quote in WSOC candidate guide: 'I am unwavering in my support of law enforcement' — affirmative advocacy beyond routine budget votes.

1.note: Campaign lists Everytown for Gun Safety and Moms Demand Action endorsements — single-issue gun-control endorsements.

profile.religion: Baptist (self-stated member, St. Luke Missionary Baptist Church)
profile.background: Community organizer (Southern Empowerment Project; Grassroots Leadership prison-reform/immigration work); Charlotte's first openly LGBT elected official (2011); District 3 2011–2019; lost 2019 at-large Dem primary; returned at-large Sept 2022; re-elected 2023 and Nov 2025 (4th of four at-large winners). Leads the city's Faith in Housing church-land affordable-housing initiative.

lawana-slack-mayfield.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: re-elected at-large Nov 4 2025, 19.11% (WFAE/Ballotpedia). HARD evidence: on council for the June 24 2019 noise ordinance (8-3) restricting amplified pro-life protest within 150 ft of medical facilities — all three no votes named (Driggs, Bokhari, Phipps), so her yes is by elimination (WFAE, ordinance No. 9585; supporters framed it as protecting abortion access — sanctity_of_life FALSE x2). Voted YES on the Feb 2016 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance (7-4; the four no votes named — Biblical Recorder) and publicly defended it against HB2 ('took away the rights of more than 90% of the population' — HuffPost 2016); Charlotte's first openly LGBT council member; endorsed by Equality NC PAC, LGBTQ+ Victory Fund, and LPAC (biblical_marriage FALSE x3). March 2018 tweet: Being Black in America 'has created homegrown terrorist wearing blue uniforms' (WFAE/WSOC) — documented contrary statement, public_justice q0 FALSE (she later said she remains 'one of the strongest supporters of law enforcement,' noted). Nov 2025 federal ICE operation: urged residents to aid affected immigrant families ('do the grocery shopping, pick up the meds' — WCCB) and joined the unanimous Nov 24 2025 vote directing $100K to nonprofits serving affected families (border q1 FALSE; no formal sanctuary stance possible under NC law, q2 null). Voted NO on the June 2026 FY2027 tax-increase budget (one of two no votes) but her rationale is undocumented — economic_stewardship left null rather than credited. NOT on council for the 2020 defund debates or the Aug 2021 NDO (out of office Dec 2019–Sept 2022). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on school choice/parental rights, prayer/displays, elections, or gun cells — null.

0.note: Voted with the 8-3 majority (June 24 2019) for the noise-ordinance buffer restricting amplified pro-life protest near the Latrobe Dr. abortion clinic; yes established by elimination — all three no votes named (Driggs, Bokhari, Phipps).

1.note: Same 2019 buffer-zone vote, framed by supporters as protecting abortion clinic access — documented contrary to abolition advocacy.

0.note: Voted YES on the Feb 2016 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance (7-4; the four no votes named); Charlotte's first openly LGBT council member, endorsed by LGBTQ+ Victory Fund.

2.note: 2016 ordinance extended gender-identity protections (incl. facilities access); she defended it against HB2 — 'took away the rights of more than 90% of the population.'

4.note: Endorsed by Equality NC PAC (2022, 2023), LGBTQ+ Victory Fund, and LPAC — accepted single-issue LGBT-advocacy endorsements.

2.note: Voted NO on the FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent tax increase (one of two no votes), but no stated rationale was found — not credited as fiscal restraint without one.

0.note: March 2018 tweet: Being Black in America 'has created homegrown terrorist wearing blue uniforms' — documented contrary statement; she later stated she remains a strong supporter of law enforcement, noted for fairness.

1.note: Nov 2025: urged residents to materially aid families affected by the federal enforcement operation and joined the unanimous vote directing $100K to nonprofits serving them.

profile.religion: Christian (self-described churchgoer; family rooted ~50 yrs at Mother Emanuel AME, Charleston)
profile.background: Johnson C. Smith Univ. grad; Bank of America/Time Warner Cable career; exec. director of Beds for Kids; Charlotte council 1999–2005, NC Senate D-40 2005–2014, lost 2014 NC-12 Dem primary; returned to council Dec 2019, re-elected unopposed Nov 2025. His sister Cynthia Graham Hurd was among the nine killed in the 2015 Mother Emanuel AME shooting — the defining event behind his gun-policy advocacy and 2025 book 'The Way Forward.'

malcolm-graham.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: re-elected unopposed Nov 4 2025, District 2 (Ballotpedia/WBTV). Scored on his REAL roll-call record (former NC Senator) plus council record. HARD evidence: NC Senate roll calls — NO on the HB 854 'Woman's Right to Know' veto override (ultrasound/24-hr wait), 7/28/2011 (sanctity_of_life FALSE x2); NO on SB 514 marriage amendment (one man/one woman), 9/13/2011 (biblical_marriage q0 FALSE); NO on HB 589 voter ID, 7/25/2013 (election_integrity q1 FALSE); NO on HB 937 expanded concealed carry, 7/23/2013, plus sustained 'common-sense gun legislation'/Charleston-loophole advocacy after his sister's murder (self_defense FALSE x2). AYE on SB 8 lifting the charter-school cap, 2/24/2011 — one of only two Democrats in favor, a documented school-choice alignment (family_child_sovereignty q0 TRUE; charter-specific). Council: yes on the unanimous Aug 2021 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance — 'building a community... with equal access for all' (WFAE; biblical_marriage q2+q4 FALSE); YES on the June 2026 FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent tax increase (9-2, by elimination; economic_stewardship FALSE). Nov 2025 federal ICE operation: 'Deploying armed, masked federal agents into our neighborhoods... is a recipe for fear,' 'There is no evidence that Charlotte faces an immigration crisis' (WBTV/CNN; border q1 FALSE; no sanctuary-policy stance, q2 null). public_justice q0 null — mixed: voted for police-raise budgets but a 2021 Charlotte Post profile says he supported rerouting CMPD budget to community programs; no affirmative pro-police advocacy found. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on prayer/displays or state-overreach cells — null.

0.note: NC Senate 7/28/2011: voted NO on the veto override of HB 854 (ultrasound + 24-hr waiting period before abortion) — documented contrary roll call.

1.note: Same roll call — voted against even modest abortion restrictions; documented contrary to abolition advocacy.

0.note: NC Senate 9/13/2011: voted NO on SB 514, the constitutional amendment defining marriage as one man and one woman (became Amendment 1).

2.note: Voted yes on the unanimous Aug 9 2021 ordinance adding gender identity/expression protections, speaking in support: 'equal access for all.'

4.note: Same 2021 SOGI ordinance vote and supporting statement — supports LGBTQ+ protections in public policy.

0.note: NC Senate 2/24/2011: AYE on SB 8 removing the charter-school cap — one of only two Democrats in favor; documented (charter-specific) school-choice alignment.

2.note: Voted YES on FY2027 budget with 1.89-cent property-tax increase, June 8 2026 (9-2; only no votes Driggs and Mayfield — yes by elimination).

1.note: NC Senate 7/25/2013: voted NO on HB 589, the photo voter-ID law.

0.note: Mixed: voted for police-raise budgets (routine governance) but a 2021 profile reports he supported rerouting CMPD budget toward community programs (secondhand paraphrase). No affirmative pro-police advocacy and no hard defund statement — null.

1.note: Nov 2025 on the federal enforcement operation: 'Deploying armed, masked federal agents into our neighborhoods is not the way to build safer streets'; 'There is no evidence that Charlotte faces an immigration crisis.'

0.note: NC Senate 7/23/2013: voted NO on HB 937 conference report expanding concealed carry — documented contrary to permitless-carry alignment.

1.note: Sustained 'common-sense gun legislation' advocacy (closing the Charleston loophole) after his sister's murder; campaign platform lists gun laws as a core issue.

profile.background: Founder/CEO of Triumph Services (brain-injury/trauma-survivor nonprofit); ~30 yrs human services, 16 in affordable/transitional housing; BA business, MA human services. On council since Dec 2019; re-elected 2022, 2023, and Nov 2025 (beat Wil Russell in the Sept 2025 primary). Frequent internal critic of city process/transparency; publicly strained relationship with Mayor Lyles. Led the May 2026 I-77 toll-rescission vote.

renee-perkins-johnson.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: won District 4 Nov 4 2025; term to Dec 2027 (Ballotpedia/WBTV); still active June 2026 (led the I-77 rescission May 11 2026). HARD evidence: sitting member for the unanimous Aug 9 2021 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance (WBTV/WFAE; biblical_marriage q2+q4 FALSE). Voted YES on the June 2026 FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent property-tax increase (9-2; only Driggs and Mayfield no — yes by elimination; economic_stewardship FALSE) — mixed record noted: she was one of three NO votes on the tax-raising FY2025 budget in June 2024 (WFAE). June 2020: publicly endorsed the council's 9-2 action halting CMPD chemical-agent purchases — the action was 'to build relationships to show that there are policies, that there is accountability' (WBTV; public_justice q0 FALSE; no affirmative pro-police advocacy found — her Firefighters Local 660 endorsement is fire, not police). Led and won (6-5) the May 2026 rescission of city support for the state-backed I-77 toll project — 'The people have spoken, and the people have won,' calling it 'a David versus Goliath moment' (WFAE; refuse_state_overreach q3 TRUE — documented subsidiarity assertion; q0 null since a toll contract is not an unconstitutional mandate). Joined the unanimous Nov 24 2025 vote directing $100K + a know-your-rights campaign for families affected by the federal ICE operation (WFAE/QCNews; border q1 FALSE — collective unanimous vote; no individual statement found). 2025 Jennings-settlement controversy: voted yes in closed session, then tried to switch to no and pushed 'stronger accountability and internal reforms' — transparency dispute, not scored. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, school choice/parental rights, religion/prayer, elections, or gun cells — null. Religion not publicly stated.

2.note: Sitting member for the unanimous Aug 9 2021 ordinance adding gender identity/expression to protected classes — recorded unanimous vote.

4.note: Same unanimous 2021 SOGI ordinance vote — supports LGBTQ+ protections in public policy.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent property-tax increase (9-2; yes by elimination). Mixed record: voted NO on the tax-raising FY2025 budget in June 2024.

0.note: June 2020: publicly endorsed the council action halting funding of CMPD chemical crowd-control agents — 'to build relationships to show that there are policies, that there is accountability.' No affirmative pro-police advocacy found.

3.note: Led and won the May 2026 rescission of city support for the state-backed I-77 toll project, explicitly on local-voice grounds: 'The people have spoken' — 'a David versus Goliath moment.'

1.note: Joined the unanimous Nov 24 2025 council vote directing $100K to nonprofits aiding families affected by the federal enforcement operation, plus a know-your-rights campaign (collective unanimous vote; no individual statement found).

profile.religion: Christian (serves on Elevation Church Outreach Team, per campaign bio)
profile.background: Licensed Professional Engineer, PMP; BS mech. engineering (Florida), MS eng. management + PhD infrastructure/environmental systems (UNC Charlotte); small business owner; daughter of two retired Army NCOs. District 3 2019–2023, at-large since Dec 2023; re-elected Nov 2025 (2nd of four at-large winners); chairs the Housing, Safety and Community Committee; lost the Dec 2025 mayor pro tem contest to Danté Anderson.

victoria-watlington.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: re-elected at-large Nov 4 2025, 20.73% (Axios/WBTV/Ballotpedia); no evidence of a run for any other office in 2026. HARD evidence: voted yes on the unanimous Aug 9 2021 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance, with a recorded comment distinguishing identity from expression while supporting the ordinance (WBTV/WFAE; biblical_marriage q2+q4 FALSE). Voted YES on both recent tax-raising budgets — FY2025 (June 2024, 8-3) and the FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent increase (June 2026, 9-2; dissenters named Driggs and Mayfield — yes by elimination) — acknowledging 'our residents are going to have to bear that burden' (WCNC; economic_stewardship FALSE). Joined the unanimous Nov 24 2025 vote directing $100K to nonprofits aiding families affected by the federal ICE operation (border q1 FALSE — collective unanimous vote; no individual statement found). public_justice null — campaign credits her with drafting the SAFE Charlotte (2020 reform-era) framework, she chairs the committee that advanced 2025 public-order ordinances, and she protested the secret CMPD-chief settlement as 'unethical, immoral, and, frankly, illegal' (a transparency dispute) — mixed, no affirmative pro-police advocacy and no defund statement. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, school choice/parental rights, prayer/displays, elections, state-overreach, or gun cells — null (her teen-gun-violence remark about losing a nephew is personal, not policy).

2.note: Voted yes on the unanimous Aug 9 2021 ordinance adding gender identity/expression protections, with recorded supportive remarks.

4.note: Same unanimous 2021 SOGI ordinance vote — supports LGBTQ+ protections in public policy.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent property-tax increase (9-2; yes by elimination) and on the tax-raising FY2025 budget (June 2024).

1.note: Joined the unanimous Nov 24 2025 council vote directing $100K + a know-your-rights campaign for families affected by the federal enforcement operation (collective unanimous vote; no individual statement found).
